"""
Fine-tunes a student model on SWE-smith agentic trajectories (Rust/ripgrep tasks).

Supports multiple teacher models:
  - gpt-5-mini:          AlienKevin/SWE-smith-rs-gpt-5-mini-trajectories                (3953 samples)
  - minimax-m2.5:        AlienKevin/SWE-smith-rs-minimax-m2.5-trajectories              (5251 samples)
  - gemini-3-flash:      AlienKevin/SWE-smith-rs-gemini-3-flash-trajectories            (1449 samples)
  - glm-4.6:             AlienKevin/SWE-smith-rs-glm-4.6-trajectories                    (369 samples)
  - swebm-minimax-m2.5:  AlienKevin/SWE-bench-multilingual-minimax-m2.5-trajectories     (299 samples)

Supports multiple student models:
  - qwen3-8b:                   Qwen/Qwen3-8B
  - qwen25-coder-32b-instruct:  Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-32B-Instruct

Each dataset has a JSON-serialized "messages" column in OpenAI chat format
plus metadata like instance_id, resolved, model, traj_id, and patch.

Usage:
    python -m experiments.exp2956_sft_swe_smith --teacher gpt-5-mini
    python -m experiments.exp2956_sft_swe_smith --teacher minimax-m2.5 --student qwen25-coder-32b-instruct
    python -m experiments.exp2956_sft_swe_smith --teacher gemini-3-flash
    python -m experiments.exp2956_sft_swe_smith --teacher glm-4.6
    python -m experiments.exp2956_sft_swe_smith --teacher swebm-minimax-m2.5

Full command for minimax-m2.5:
RAY_AUTH_MODE=token uv run lib/marin/src/marin/run/ray_run.py \\
    --env_vars WANDB_API_KEY ${WANDB_API_KEY} \\
    --env_vars WANDB_ENTITY marin-community \\
    --env_vars WANDB_PROJECT marin \\
    --env_vars TPU_CI true \\
    --env_vars HF_TOKEN ${HF_TOKEN} \\
    --cluster us-east5-a \\
    -- python -m experiments.exp2956_sft_swe_smith --teacher minimax-m2.5 --force_run_failed true
"""

import argparse
import dataclasses
import math
from dataclasses import dataclass

from levanter.data.text import ChatLmDatasetFormat
from levanter.layers.rotary import DefaultRotaryEmbeddingsConfig

from experiments.defaults import default_sft, default_tokenize
from experiments.posttrain.instruction_datasets import (
    INSTRUCTION_DATASET_NAME_TO_CONFIG,
    get_instruction_dataset,
)
from experiments.qwen2pt5_instruct_chat_template import QWEN_2_5_INSTRUCT_CHAT_TEMPLATE
from experiments.qwen3 import (
    qwen2_5_coder_32b_instruct,
    qwen2_5_coder_32b_instruct_tokenizer,
    qwen3_8b,
    qwen3_8b_tokenizer,
)
from experiments.qwen3_chat_template import QWEN_3_CHAT_TEMPLATE
from experiments.simple_sft_config import SimpleSFTConfig, compute_per_device_parallelism
from fray.cluster import ResourceConfig
from marin.execution.executor import ExecutorStep, executor_main
from marin.processing.tokenize import TokenizerStep, lm_mixture_data_config


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class TeacherConfig:
    """Configuration for a teacher model's SWE-smith trajectory dataset."""

    hf_dataset_id: str
    num_samples: int


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class StudentConfig:
    """Configuration for a student model to fine-tune."""

    model_config: object
    tokenizer: str
    model_name_or_path: str
    chat_template: str


TEACHER_CONFIGS: dict[str, TeacherConfig] = {
    "gpt-5-mini": TeacherConfig(
        hf_dataset_id="AlienKevin/SWE-smith-rs-gpt-5-mini-trajectories",
        num_samples=3953,
    ),
    "minimax-m2.5": TeacherConfig(
        hf_dataset_id="AlienKevin/SWE-smith-rs-minimax-m2.5-trajectories",
        num_samples=5251,
    ),
    "gemini-3-flash": TeacherConfig(
        hf_dataset_id="AlienKevin/SWE-smith-rs-gemini-3-flash-trajectories",
        num_samples=1449,
    ),
    "glm-4.6": TeacherConfig(
        hf_dataset_id="AlienKevin/SWE-smith-rs-glm-4.6-trajectories",
        num_samples=369,
    ),
    "swebm-minimax-m2.5": TeacherConfig(
        hf_dataset_id="AlienKevin/SWE-bench-multilingual-minimax-m2.5-trajectories",
        num_samples=299,
    ),
}

STUDENT_CONFIGS: dict[str, StudentConfig] = {
    "qwen3-8b": StudentConfig(
        model_config=qwen3_8b,
        tokenizer=qwen3_8b_tokenizer,
        model_name_or_path="Qwen/Qwen3-8B",
        chat_template=QWEN_3_CHAT_TEMPLATE,
    ),
    "qwen25-coder-32b-instruct": StudentConfig(
        model_config=qwen2_5_coder_32b_instruct,
        tokenizer=qwen2_5_coder_32b_instruct_tokenizer,
        model_name_or_path="Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-32B-Instruct",
        chat_template=QWEN_2_5_INSTRUCT_CHAT_TEMPLATE,
    ),
}

TARGET_EPOCHS = 3
TRAIN_BATCH_SIZE = 16
MICROBATCH_SIZE = 16
RESOURCES = ResourceConfig.with_tpu("v5p-32")
RESOURCE_SUFFIX = RESOURCES.device.variant.replace("-", "") if RESOURCES.device.kind == "tpu" else "gpu"


def _create_tokenization_step(
    dataset_identifier: str, short_name: str, tokenizer: str, chat_template: str
) -> TokenizerStep:
    dataset_config = INSTRUCTION_DATASET_NAME_TO_CONFIG[dataset_identifier]
    dataset = get_instruction_dataset(dataset_identifier, splits=dataset_config.splits)
    return default_tokenize(
        name=f"{short_name.split('/')[-1]}_qwen3_8b_tokenizer",
        dataset=dataset / "**/*.jsonl.gz",
        tokenizer=tokenizer,
        format=ChatLmDatasetFormat(chat_template=chat_template),
    )


def build_swe_smith_sft(teacher: str, student: str = "qwen3-8b") -> tuple[ExecutorStep, ExecutorStep]:
    """Build SFT experiment and checkpoint steps for a given teacher and student model.

    Args:
        teacher: One of the keys in TEACHER_CONFIGS.
        student: One of the keys in STUDENT_CONFIGS (default: "qwen3-8b").

    Returns:
        (sft_step, checkpoint_step) tuple.
    """
    teacher_config = TEACHER_CONFIGS[teacher]
    student_config = STUDENT_CONFIGS[student]

    model_config = dataclasses.replace(
        student_config.model_config,
        max_seq_len=32768,
        rope=DefaultRotaryEmbeddingsConfig(theta=1_000_000.0),
        cross_entropy_block_size=32000,  # Process vocab in chunks to reduce memory during loss computation
    )

    datasets = {teacher_config.hf_dataset_id: teacher_config.hf_dataset_id}
    weights = {teacher_config.hf_dataset_id: float(teacher_config.num_samples)}

    tokenized_datasets = {
        name: _create_tokenization_step(dataset_id, name, student_config.tokenizer, student_config.chat_template)
        for name, dataset_id in datasets.items()
    }

    total_examples = teacher_config.num_samples
    num_train_steps = math.ceil(TARGET_EPOCHS * total_examples / TRAIN_BATCH_SIZE)

    warmup_steps = 5
    warmup_fraction = warmup_steps / num_train_steps if num_train_steps > 0 else 0.0

    sft_config = SimpleSFTConfig(
        resources=RESOURCES,
        tokenizer=student_config.tokenizer,
        model_name_or_path=student_config.model_name_or_path,
        train_batch_size=TRAIN_BATCH_SIZE,
        per_device_parallelism=compute_per_device_parallelism(TRAIN_BATCH_SIZE, MICROBATCH_SIZE, RESOURCES),
        per_device_eval_parallelism=8,
        num_train_steps=num_train_steps,
        learning_rate=1e-4,
        max_seq_len=32768,
        seed=42,
        steps_per_checkpoint=(total_examples // TRAIN_BATCH_SIZE) // 4,  # Every quarter epoch
        lr_schedule="cosine",
        warmup=warmup_fraction,
        weight_decay=0.01,
        beta1=0.9,
        beta2=0.999,
        epsilon=1e-8,
        pad_tokenizer_to_match_model=True,  # Model and tokenizer vocab sizes differ
    )

    mixture_config = lm_mixture_data_config(
        tokenized_datasets,
        weights,
        permutation_type="feistel",
        # IMPORTANT: Era shuffling (shuffle after every epoch). `shuffle=True` repeats the same shuffle each epoch.
        shuffle=total_examples,
        missing_weights_are_validation=True,
        mixture_block_size=12288,  # Doesn't matter for mixtures with 1 dataset
    )

    # Sanitize names for use in experiment name (e.g., "minimax-m2.5" -> "minimax_m2_5")
    teacher_slug = teacher.replace("-", "_").replace(".", "_")
    student_slug = student.replace("-", "_").replace(".", "_")

    sft_step = default_sft(
        name=f"exp2956_sft_swe_smith_{teacher_slug}_{student_slug}_32768tokens_{RESOURCE_SUFFIX}",
        tokenized=mixture_config,
        model_config=model_config,
        sft_config=sft_config,
        tags=["qwen", "swe-smith", "sft", teacher, student, RESOURCE_SUFFIX],
    )

    checkpoint = sft_step.cd(f"hf/step-{num_train_steps - 1}").nonblocking()

    return sft_step, checkpoint


# Build all teacher experiments at module level for importability (default student: qwen3-8b)
exp2956_sft_swe_smith_qwen3_8b, exp2956_checkpoint = build_swe_smith_sft("gpt-5-mini")
exp2956_sft_swe_smith_minimax_qwen3_8b, exp2956_minimax_checkpoint = build_swe_smith_sft("minimax-m2.5")
exp2956_sft_swe_smith_gemini_qwen3_8b, exp2956_gemini_checkpoint = build_swe_smith_sft("gemini-3-flash")
exp2956_sft_swe_smith_glm_qwen3_8b, exp2956_glm_checkpoint = build_swe_smith_sft("glm-4.6")
exp2956_sft_swe_smith_swebm_minimax_qwen3_8b, exp2956_swebm_minimax_checkpoint = build_swe_smith_sft(
    "swebm-minimax-m2.5"
)


if __name__ == "__main__":
    import sys

    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(description="SFT on SWE-smith trajectories")
    parser.add_argument(
        "--teacher",
        choices=list(TEACHER_CONFIGS.keys()),
        default="gpt-5-mini",
        help="Teacher model whose trajectories to train on (default: gpt-5-mini)",
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "--student",
        choices=list(STUDENT_CONFIGS.keys()),
        default="qwen3-8b",
        help="Student model to fine-tune (default: qwen3-8b)",
    )
    args, remaining = parser.parse_known_args()
    # Strip --teacher and --student from sys.argv so draccus in executor_main only sees executor args
    sys.argv = [sys.argv[0], *remaining]
    sft_step, _ = build_swe_smith_sft(args.teacher, args.student)
    executor_main(steps=[sft_step])

lines: warmup_fraction = warmup_steps / num_train_steps if num_train_steps > 0 else 0.0
+    decay_fraction = 1.0 - warmup_fraction  # All post-warmup steps are cosine decay (no stable phase)

content.text:
As we suspected — **the 32B model OOMs on v5p-32**. Here's the sequence:

1. **Attempt 1**: Resumed training from step 46 → all 4 TPU nodes crashed (OOM, marked dead)
2. **Attempt 2**: Framework retried on new nodes, currently loading checkpoint step-45 again — likely will OOM again

The step-45 checkpoint exists because the first failed run (with `decay=0`) got through model loading and checkpointing before crashing at optimizer build. The actual forward+backward pass on a training batch is what OOMs.

**Bottom line**: v5p-32 (16 chips, ~1.5TB HBM) is not enough for full finetuning of Qwen2.5-Coder-32B at 32768 seq len. You'll need at least **v5p-64** (32 chips), which is what the other 32B experiments in this repo use. Want me to add a per-student resource override?

message.content: How to prevent OOM with 32b on v5p-32?

content.thinking:
The user wants to know how to prevent OOM when training a 32B model on v5p-32. Let me think about the options:

1. **Reduce sequence length** - From 32768 to 16384 or 8192. This directly reduces activation memory.
2. **Gradient checkpointing** - Recompute activations during backward pass instead of storing them. This is a common technique for large models.
3. **Reduce cross_entropy_block_size** - Already set to 32000, could reduce further.
4. **Use a larger TPU** - v5p-64 or v5p-128.


5. **Enable activation checkpointing** - I should check if Levanter supports this like torchtune does, since it could significantly reduce memory usage during training.

content.text: Let me check what memory-saving options are available in Levanter/the model configs.

input.prompt:
Search in /home/kevin/marin-agentic-sft for how gradient checkpointing (also called activation checkpointing or remat) is configured. Look in:
1. Levanter trainer config options (lib/levanter/src/levanter/) - search for "gradient_checkpointing", "activation_checkpointing", "remat"
2. SimpleSFTConfig or any SFT config that passes these options
3. The QwenConfig or model configs for any memory-saving flags
4. Any existing 32B experiments that use gradient checkpointing (check exp1295, exp1395, tootsie experiments)

I need to understand: what options exist to reduce memory usage for training (gradient checkpointing, activation offloading, reduced precision, etc.) and how they're configured in this framework.

    79→def main(fn=None, *, args: Optional[List[str]] = None, config_dir: Optional[str] = DEFAULT_CONFIG_DIR):
    80→    """
    81→    Like draccus.wrap but can handle config paths that are urls loadable by fsspec.
    82→    This isn't documented in levanter.config.main_decorator, but only the first arg can be config-ified.
    83→
    84→    :param args: the args to parse. If None, will use sys.argv[1:]
    85→    :param config_dir: the directory to look for configs in (if the path does not exist already). If None, will only use the current working directory
    86→    """
    87→
    88→    if fn is None:
    89→        return functools.partial(main, args=args, config_dir=config_dir)
    90→
    91→    _cmdline_args = args
    92→    if args is None:
    93→        _cmdline_args = sys.argv[1:]
    94→
    95→    @wraps(fn)
    96→    def wrapper_inner(*args, **kwargs):
    97→        config_path, cmdline_args = _maybe_get_config_path_and_cmdline_args(_cmdline_args)
    98→        paths_to_check = [config_path, f"{config_path}.yaml", f"{config_path}.yml"]
    99→        if config_path is not None and config_dir is not None:
   100→            paths_to_check.extend([os.path.join(config_dir, p) for p in paths_to_check])
   101→
   102→        for path in paths_to_check:
   103→            if path is not None and os.path.exists(path):
   104→                config_path = path
   105→                break
   106→
   107→        argspec = inspect.getfullargspec(fn)
   108→        argtype = argspec.annotations[argspec.args[0]]
   109→        cfg = parse(config_class=argtype, config_path=config_path, args=cmdline_args)
   110→        response = fn(cfg, *args, **kwargs)
   111→        return response
   112→
   113→    return wrapper_inner
   114→
   115→
   116→def _maybe_get_config_path_and_cmdline_args(args: List[str]):
   117→    """
   118→    We want to accept ... --config_path <config> ... where config could be a path or url.
   119→    If URL, we need to download it and save it to a temp file. We then want to remove --config_path
   120→    from the cmdline args so that draccus doesn't try to load it as a config path and return it separately here
   121→    along with the modified cmdline args.
   122→    We also accept ... --configs <config1> <config2> ... and concatenate them into a single config.
   123→    """
   124→    if "--config_path" not in args and "--config" not in args and "--configs" not in args:
   125→        return None, args
   126→    else:
   127→        config_args = ["--config_path", "--config", "--configs"]
   128→        found_indices = [args.index(arg) for arg in config_args if arg in args]
   129→        if len(found_indices) > 1:
   130→            raise ValueError(f"Multiple config args found in {args}")
   131→        config_path_index = found_indices[0] + 1
   132→
   133→        config_paths: List[str] = []
   134→        args = args.copy()
   135→        del args[config_path_index - 1]
   136→        config_path_index -= 1
   137→
   138→        while config_path_index < len(args) and not args[config_path_index].startswith("-"):
   139→
   140→            config_path = args[config_path_index]
   141→
   142→            if urllib.parse.urlparse(config_path).scheme:
   143→                fs: AbstractFileSystem
   144→                fs, fs_path = fsspec.core.url_to_fs(config_path)
   145→                temp_file = tempfile.NamedTemporaryFile(prefix="config", suffix=".yaml", delete=False)
   146→                atexit.register(lambda: os.unlink(temp_file.name))
   147→                fs.get(fs_path, temp_file.name)
   148→                config_path = temp_file.name
   149→
   150→            config_paths.append(config_path)
   151→            del args[config_path_index]
   152→
   153→        merged_config_path = None
   154→
   155→        if len(config_paths) == 1:
   156→            merged_config_path = config_paths[0]
   157→        elif len(config_paths) > 1:
   158→            # merge the configs by concatenating them
   159→            temp_merged_config_path = tempfile.NamedTemporaryFile(prefix="config_merged", suffix=".yaml", delete=False)
   160→            atexit.register(lambda: os.unlink(temp_merged_config_path.name))
   161→            with open(temp_merged_config_path.name, "w") as f:
   162→                for config_path in config_paths:
   163→                    with open(config_path) as config_file:
   164→                        f.write(config_file.read())
   165→            merged_config_path = temp_merged_config_path.name
   166→        else:
   167→            raise ValueError("No config path found in args")
   168→
   169→        return merged_config_path, args
   170→

   357→    def __enter__(self):

   364→            hax.axis_mapping(self.parameter_axis_mapping),

   367→        for cmanager in self._cmanagers:

   372→    def __exit__(self, *args):
   373→        problems = []

   377→            except Exception as e:
   378→                problems.append(e)
   379→
   380→        self._cmanagers = []
   381→
   382→        if len(problems) > 0:
   383→            raise RuntimeError("Exception(s) occurred while exiting trainer", problems) from problems[0]

   402→                parameters for the optimizer state and for computing gradients. Non-trainable parameters are also
   403→                not checkpointed. If you don't specify this, all parameters are assumed to be trainable.

   411→        assert model_init is not None

   414→        checkpoint_path = self.checkpoint_path

   467→    def train_step(self, state: S, *batch: X, **batch_kwargs) -> StepInfo[S]:
   468→        """
   469→        Performs a single training step.
   470→        """
   471→        # jit hooks impose a nontrivial cost even when they're not run (since they defeat some compiler optimizations)
   472→        # so we avoid running them when they're not needed
   473→        # this results in two compiles, but the cost of the second compile is worth it
   474→        hooks_this_time = any(state.step % h.every == 0 for h in self.hooks.jit_hooks)
   475→
   476→        with capture_time() as step_time:
   477→            if hooks_this_time:
   478→                result = self._maybe_save_jaxpr("train_step", self._jit_train_step_fn, state, batch, batch_kwargs)
   479→                # force the loss so timing numbers are accurate. laziness isn't going to help here (i think?)
   480→            else:
   481→                result = self._maybe_save_jaxpr(
   482→                    "train_step_hooks", self._jit_train_step_fn_no_hook, state, batch, batch_kwargs
   483→                )
   484→
   485→            loss = result.loss.item()
   486→
   487→            if self.config.crash_on_nan and jnp.isnan(loss):
   488→                raise RuntimeError("Loss is NaN")
   489→
   490→            if self.config.crash_on_inf and jnp.isinf(loss):
   491→                raise RuntimeError("Loss is Inf")
   492→
   493→            info = StepInfo(result.new_state, loss, step_time())
   494→
   495→            with capture_time() as hook_time:
   496→                self.run_hooks(info)
   497→                if hooks_this_time:
   498→                    self.hooks.run_jit_hooks_outside_step(info, result.hook_infos)
   499→
   500→            # Log metrics from loss function and throughput metrics
   501→            metrics_to_log = {**result.loss_metrics, "throughput/hook_time": hook_time()}
   502→            levanter.tracker.log(metrics_to_log, step=info.step)
   503→
   504→        return info
   505→
   506→    def training_steps(self, state: S, train_loader) -> typing.Iterator[StepInfo[S]]:
   507→        """
   508→        Generator that yields training steps and runs hooks.
   509→        """
   510→        iter_data = iter(train_loader)
   511→
   512→        while int(state.step) < self.num_train_steps:
   513→            with capture_time() as loading_time:
   514→                try:
   515→                    example = next(iter_data)
   516→                except StopIteration:
   517→                    logger.info("Reached end of training data loader")
   518→                    break
   519→            info = self.train_step(state, example)
   520→            state = info.state
   521→
   522→            levanter.tracker.log({"throughput/loading_time": loading_time()}, step=info.step)
   523→
   524→            yield info
   525→
   526→    def train(self, state: S, train_loader: Iterable[X]) -> StepInfo[S]:
   527→        """
   528→        Performs training until the number of steps is reached.
   529→        """
   530→        info: Optional[StepInfo[S]] = None
   531→        for info in self.training_steps(state, train_loader):
   532→            pass
   533→
   534→        if info is None:
   535→            raise RuntimeError(
   536→                "No training steps were executed. The dataset may be empty or there are no steps left to run."
   537→            )
   538→
   539→        # force hooks to run at the end
   540→        self.run_hooks(info, force=True)
   541→
   542→        return info

   544→    def _add_default_hooks(self):
   545→        from levanter import callbacks

   547→        self.add_hook(levanter.callbacks.pbar_logger(total=self.config.num_train_steps), every=1)

   550→        checkpointer = self.config.checkpointer.create(self.run_id)

   552→
   553→        # Add watch callback if configured

   556→
   557→        if self.config.profiler:

   559→            total_prof_steps = self.config.profiler_num_steps
   560→            if total_prof_steps + self.config.profiler_start_step > self.config.num_train_steps:
   561→                logger.warning(
   562→                    f"Adjusting profiler_total_steps from {total_prof_steps} to"
   563→                    f" {self.config.num_train_steps - self.config.profiler_start_step}"
   564→                )
   565→                total_prof_steps = self.config.num_train_steps - self.config.profiler_start_step
   566→            self.add_hook(
   567→                callbacks.profile(

   569→                    self.config.profiler_start_step,
   570→                    total_prof_steps,
   571→                    self.config.profiler_perfetto_link,
   572→                ),
   573→                every=1,

    65→def _normalize_unroll(unroll: int | bool | None, block_size: int) -> int | bool:
    66→    """Convert user-provided ``unroll`` values into something understood by ``jax.lax.scan``."""
    67→
    68→    if unroll is None:
    69→        return 1
    70→
    71→    if isinstance(unroll, bool):
    72→        return unroll
    73→
    74→    resolved = int(unroll)
    75→    if resolved < 1:
    76→        raise ValueError(f"unroll must be >= 1; got {resolved}.")
    77→
    78→    return resolved

    81→class BlockFoldable(Protocol[M]):
    82→    """Common interface for :class:`~haliax.nn.Stacked` and :class:`~haliax.nn.BlockSeq`.
    83→
    84→    The interface exposes the :py:meth:`fold` and :py:meth:`scan` methods along with the helper
    85→    methods :py:meth:`fold_via`, :py:meth:`scan_via`, and :py:meth:`vmap_via`.
    86→
    87→    This is a protocol, so you can use it as a type hint for a function that takes a ``Stacked`` or ``BlockSeq``.
    88→    Equinox modules can't directly inherit from ``Protocol`` classes, but you can use it as a type hint.
    89→    """
    90→
    91→    Block: Axis
    92→
    93→    @classmethod
    94→    def init(
    95→        cls: Type[S],
    96→        Block: Axis,
    97→        module: Type[M],
    98→        *,
    99→        gradient_checkpointing: ScanCheckpointSpec = False,
   100→        prevent_cse: bool = False,
   101→    ) -> ModuleInit[S]: ...
   102→
   103→    def scan(self, init: T, *extra_args, unroll: int | bool | None = None, **extra_kwargs): ...
   104→
   105→    def fold(self, init: T, *args, unroll: int | bool | None = None, **kwargs) -> T: ...
   106→
   107→    @overload
   108→    def fold_via(
   109→        self, fn: FoldFunction[M, P, CarryT], *, unroll: int | bool | None = None
   110→    ) -> Callable[Concatenate[CarryT, P], CarryT]: ...
   111→
   112→    @overload
   113→    def fold_via(
   114→        self, fn: Callable[[M, CarryT], CarryT], *, unroll: int | bool | None = None
   115→    ) -> Callable[[CarryT], CarryT]: ...
   116→
   117→    def fold_via(
   118→        self, fn: Callable[..., CarryT], *, unroll: int | bool | None = None
   119→    ) -> Callable[Concatenate[CarryT, P], CarryT]: ...
   120→
   121→    @overload
   122→    def scan_via(
   123→        self, fn: ScanFunction[M, CarryT, P, OutputT_co], *, unroll: int | bool | None = None
   124→    ) -> Callable[Concatenate[CarryT, P], tuple[CarryT, OutputT_co]]: ...
   125→
   126→    @overload
   127→    def scan_via(
   128→        self,
   129→        fn: Callable[[M, CarryT], tuple[CarryT, OutputT_co]],
   130→        *,
   131→        unroll: int | bool | None = None,
   132→    ) -> Callable[[CarryT], tuple[CarryT, OutputT_co]]: ...
   133→
   134→    def scan_via(
   135→        self, fn: Callable[..., tuple[CarryT, OutputT_co]], *, unroll: int | bool | None = None
   136→    ) -> Callable[P, tuple[CarryT, OutputT_co]]: ...
   137→
   138→    @overload
   139→    def vmap_via(self, fn: VmapFunction[M, P, OutputT_co]) -> Callable[P, OutputT_co]: ...
   140→
   141→    @overload
   142→    def vmap_via(self, fn: Callable[[M], OutputT_co]) -> Callable[[], OutputT_co]: ...
   143→
   144→    def vmap_via(self, fn: Callable[..., OutputT_co]) -> Callable[..., OutputT_co]: ...
   145→
   146→    def unstacked(self) -> Sequence[M]:
   147→        """
   148→        Returns the unstacked version of this module. This is useful for logging or saving checkpoints.
   149→
   150→        """

    42→@dataclasses.dataclass(frozen=True)
    43→class ScanCheckpointPolicy:
    44→    """
    45→    A class that represents a gradient checkpoint policy for blocks in a Stacked module. This is used to control
    46→    gradient checkpointing in [haliax.scan][], [haliax.fold][], [haliax.nn.Stacked][] and [haliax.nn.BlockSeq][].
    47→
    48→    Gradient checkpointing is a technique for reducing memory usage in training large models. It works by saving only a
    49→    subset of the forward pass and recomputing the rest in the backward pass. (By doing parts of the forward pass again)
    50→    JAX suggests that this usually isn't necessary when not using scan-over-layers (i.e. Stacked), so this is mostly
    51→    useful for Stacked modules.
    52→
    53→    A scan block takes a "carry" and some extra arguments, and returns a "carry" and an "output". The "carry" is passed
    54→    to the next block, and the "output" is concatenated into a final result (sort of like an RNN).
    55→
    56→    Schematically it might look like this:
    57→
    58→    ```
    59→          I       I       I       I
    60→          |       |       |       |
    61→    C ->  B -C->  B -C->  B -C->  B --> C
    62→          |       |       |       |
    63→          O       O       O       O
    64→    ```
    65→
    66→    where "C" is the carry and "O" is the output. A block will typically do some computation (e.g. a Transformer block)
    67→    as well, which might require saving or recomputing in the backward pass.
    68→
    69→    Without checkpointing, we will save all block inputs and outputs, and use them to compute the gradient. This requires
    70→    memory, of course. With checkpointing, we can save only some of the computation. Typically, to compute the gradient
    71→    of the scan, we need the inputs to each block, the intermediates within each block, as well as the carries (which
    72→    are inputs to the next block). We can save all of these, or we can save only some of them.
    73→
    74→    With this class, you can specify if you want to save the carries, or the internals/inputs. You can also offload
    75→    the carries to the host, which can reduce memory usage on the device.
    76→
    77→    #### Nested Remat
    78→
    79→    Alternatively, we can do something a bit more clever. We can break the computation into "blocks" of size "B", and
    80→    save the carries and outputs of each block. Then, during the backward pass, we can recompute the outputs of each
    81→    block using the carries and inputs, and then compute the gradient as usual. This requires O(B) memory and O(N)
    82→    time. When B = sqrt(N), this is O(sqrt(N)) memory and O(N) time. This is the "nested scan" policy.
    83→    In practice, this is about 20% slower than the O(N) memory policy, but it can be worth it for large models.
    84→
    85→    #### Offloading
    86→
    87→    Another choice is to "offload" carries and outputs to the host, which can reduce memory usage on the device.
    88→    We support offloading carries and outputs to the host, but not internals.
    89→
    90→    See Also:
    91→        * [JAX docs on gradient checkpointing](https://docs.jax.dev/en/latest/gradient-checkpointing.html)
    92→    """
    93→
    94→    save_carries: bool | Literal["offload"] = True
    95→    """
    96→    Whether to save all carries in the forward pass. If True, (input) carries are saved in the forward pass and used in the
    97→    backward pass. If "offload", carries are saved in the forward pass and offloaded to the host
    98→
    99→    If False, carries are recomputed in the backward pass.
   100→    """
   101→
   102→    save_inputs: bool | Literal["offload"] = False
   103→    """
   104→    Whether to save all non-carry inputs in the forward pass. If True, inputs are saved in the forward pass and used in
   105→    the backward pass. If "offload", inputs are saved in the forward pass and offloaded to the host.
   106→    """
   107→
   108→    save_block_internals: bool | list[str] = False
   109→    """
   110→    Whether to save internal state of blocks. If a list, only the listed names are saved, as
   111→    with [jax.checkpoint_policies.save_only_these_names][].
   112→
   113→    See Also: https://docs.jax.dev/en/latest/gradient-checkpointing.html#custom-policies-for-offload
   114→    """
   115→
   116→    offload_block_internals: list[str] = dataclasses.field(default_factory=list)
   117→    """
   118→    List of named block internals to offload to the host. This is useful for reducing memory usage on the device
   119→    while still avoiding rematerialization.
   120→    """
   121→
   122→    prevent_cse: bool = False
   123→    """
   124→    Whether to prevent common subexpression elimination in the checkpointed function.
   125→    """
   126→
   127→    disable: bool = False
   128→    """
   129→    Whether to disable gradient checkpointing entirely. This is useful for debugging.
   130→    """
   131→
   132→    simple: bool = False
   133→    """
   134→    Whether to use the simple gradient checkpointing policy. This is useful for debugging.
   135→    """
   136→
   137→    nested: bool | int = False
   138→    """
   139→    Allows for nested remat with a double scan. We reshape the stack into [nested_remat, -1] and then scan over both
   140→    in sequence. If True, we find the closest int to sqrt(len(stack)) such that len(stack) % int == 0.
   141→    If False, we don't do anything.
   142→    """
   143→
   144→    @staticmethod
   145→    def from_bool_or_str(remat_policy: bool | str):
   146→        """
   147→        Convert a boolean or string into a BlockCheckpointPolicy. This is useful for converting user input
   148→        into a BlockCheckpointPolicy.
   149→
   150→        Choices:
   151→            * True: save outputs, don't save block internals. This is the classic Haliax behavior.
   152→            * False: save everything.
   153→            * "offload": offload outputs to the host, don't save block internals.
   154→            * "recompute" or "full": don't save outputs or block internals.
   155→            * "save_all": save outputs and block internals. Equivalent to False
   156→            * "nested": use nested remat. Equivalent to True
   157→        """
   158→        if remat_policy == "offload":
   159→            return ScanCheckpointPolicy(save_carries="offload", save_inputs="offload", save_block_internals=False)
   160→        elif remat_policy == "recompute" or remat_policy == "full":
   161→            return ScanCheckpointPolicy(save_carries=False, save_inputs=False, save_block_internals=False)
   162→        elif remat_policy == "save_all":
   163→            return ScanCheckpointPolicy(save_carries=True, save_inputs=True, save_block_internals=True)
   164→        elif remat_policy == "nested":
   165→            return ScanCheckpointPolicy(nested=True)
   166→        elif remat_policy is True:
   167→            return ScanCheckpointPolicy(simple=True)
   168→        elif remat_policy is False:
   169→            return ScanCheckpointPolicy(save_carries=True, save_inputs=True, save_block_internals=True, disable=True)
   170→        else:
   171→            raise ValueError(f"Invalid checkpoint policy {remat_policy}")
   172→
   173→    @staticmethod
   174→    def _mk(remat_policy: bool | str | "ScanCheckpointPolicy") -> "ScanCheckpointPolicy":
   175→        if isinstance(remat_policy, ScanCheckpointPolicy):
   176→            return remat_policy
   177→        else:
   178→            return ScanCheckpointPolicy.from_bool_or_str(remat_policy)
   179→
   180→    def checkpoint(self, carry_name: str, input_name: str, callable):
   181→        if self.disable:
   182→            return callable
   183→        elif self.simple:
   184→            return eqx.filter_checkpoint(callable, prevent_cse=self.prevent_cse)
   185→        else:
   186→            policy = self._to_jax_policy(carry_name, input_name)
   187→            return eqx.filter_checkpoint(callable, policy=policy, prevent_cse=self.prevent_cse)
   188→

   190→        assert isinstance(carry_name, str)
   191→        assert isinstance(input_name, str)
   192→        our_names_to_save = []
   193→        our_names_to_offload = []
   194→        our_names_to_remat = []
   195→
   196→        # return jax.checkpoint_policies.save_only_these_names(carry_name, output_name)
   197→
   198→        if self.save_inputs is True:
   199→            our_names_to_save.append(input_name)
   200→        elif self.save_inputs == "offload":

    35→
    36→
    37→@LmConfig.register_subclass("llama")
    38→@dataclass(frozen=True)
    39→class LlamaConfig(HFCompatConfig):
    40→    """Config for LlamaModel
    41→
    42→    Args:
    43→        max_seq_len (int, optional): maximum length of the input sequence. Defaults to 2048.
    44→        hidden_dim (int, optional): dimension of the hidden state. Defaults to 4096.
    45→        intermediate_dim (int, optional): dimension of the intermediate state. Defaults to 11008.
    46→        num_layers (int, optional): number of hidden layers in the Transformer encoder. Defaults to 32.
    47→        num_heads (int, optional): number of attention heads for each attention layer. Defaults to 32.
    48→        num_kv_heads (int, optional): number of attention heads for keys and values in each attention layer.
    49→            Setting to 1 means MQA. Setting to num_heads means MHA. Otherwise GQA.
    50→            Note that num_heads must be divisible by this number. Defaults to 32.
    51→        activation_function (str, optional): activation function for the hidden layer. Defaults to "silu".
    52→        hybrid_norm (bool, optional): whether to use hybrid normalization with additional layer norms after attention and MLP. Defaults to False.
    53→        input_embedding_norm (bool, optional): whether to use layer normalization after input embeddings. Defaults to False.
    54→    """
    55→
    56→    max_seq_len: int = 2048
    57→    hidden_dim: int = 4096
    58→    intermediate_dim: int = 11008
    59→    num_layers: int = 32
    60→    num_heads: int = 32
    61→    head_dim: int | None = None  # if set, will use this as the head dimension instead of hidden_dim // num_heads
    62→    num_kv_heads: int = 32
    63→    activation_function: ActivationFunctionEnum = ActivationFunctionEnum.silu
    64→    initializer_range: float = 0.02
    65→    layer_norm_epsilon: float = 1e-5
    66→    tie_word_embeddings: bool = False
    67→    hybrid_norm: bool = (
    68→        False  # if set to True, this will add an additional layer norm after the attention and MLP layers
    69→    )
    70→    use_qk_norm: bool = (
    71→        False  # if set to True, this will add an additional layer norm after the key and query projections
    72→    )
    73→    input_embedding_norm: bool = False
    74→
    75→    # Attention-related config
    76→    upcast_attn: bool = False
    77→    attn_backend: Optional[AttentionBackend] = None
    78→    flash_attention_block_size: Optional[int] = None
    79→
    80→    gradient_checkpointing: bool | ScanCheckpointPolicy | str = True
    81→    scan_layers: bool = True
    82→
    83→    use_bias: bool = False
    84→    use_layer_norm_weight: bool = True
    85→    rope: RotaryEmbeddingsConfig = dataclasses.field(default_factory=DefaultRotaryEmbeddingsConfig)
    86→
    87→    reference_checkpoint: str = "NousResearch/Llama-2-7b-hf"
    88→    tokenizer: Optional[str] = None
    89→
    90→    # Axis
    91→    @property
    92→    def KeyPos(self) -> Axis:
    93→        return self.max_Pos.alias("key_position")
    94→
    95→    @property
    96→    def Embed(self) -> Axis:
    97→        return Axis(name="embed", size=self.hidden_dim)
    98→
    99→    Layers = property(lambda self: Axis(name="layer", size=self.num_layers))
   100→    Mlp = property(lambda self: Axis(name="mlp", size=self.intermediate_dim))
   101→
   102→    def __post_init__(self):
   103→        assert (
   104→            self.num_heads % self.num_kv_heads == 0
   105→        ), f"num_heads={self.num_heads} not divisible by num_kv_heads={self.num_kv_heads}."
   106→
   107→    def hf_checkpoint_converter(self, ref_checkpoint: Optional[str] = None) -> HFCheckpointConverter["LlamaConfig"]:  # type: ignore
   108→        return HFCheckpointConverter(
   109→            self.__class__,
   110→            reference_checkpoint=self.reference_checkpoint if ref_checkpoint is None else ref_checkpoint,
   111→            trust_remote_code=True,
   112→            tokenizer=ref_checkpoint if self.tokenizer is None else self.tokenizer,
   113→            HfConfigClass=HfLlamaConfig,
   114→        )
   115→
   116→    @classmethod
   117→    def from_hf_config(cls, hf_config: HfConfig):
   118→        rope_theta = hf_config.rope_theta
   119→        rope_config = RotaryEmbeddingsConfig.from_hf_config(rope_theta, getattr(hf_config, "rope_scaling", None))
   120→        return LlamaConfig(
   121→            max_seq_len=hf_config.max_position_embeddings,
   122→            hidden_dim=hf_config.hidden_size,
   123→            intermediate_dim=hf_config.intermediate_size,
   124→            num_layers=hf_config.num_hidden_layers,
   125→            num_heads=hf_config.num_attention_heads,
   126→            num_kv_heads=hf_config.num_key_value_heads,
   127→            activation_function=ActivationFunctionEnum(hf_config.hidden_act),
   128→            initializer_range=hf_config.initializer_range,
   129→            layer_norm_epsilon=hf_config.rms_norm_eps,
   130→            tie_word_embeddings=hf_config.tie_word_embeddings,
   131→            rope=rope_config,
   132→        )
   133→
   134→    def to_hf_config(self, vocab_size: int, config_overrides: Optional[Dict] = None) -> HfLlamaConfig:
   135→        """Convert to HuggingFace's LlamaConfig
   136→
   137→        Args:
   138→            vocab_size (int, optional): Vocabulary size of the tokenizer. Defaults to 32000.
   139→            config_overrides (dict, optional): Overrides for the config. Defaults to None.
   140→
   141→        Returns:
   142→            HfLlamaConfig: HuggingFace's LlamaConfig
   143→
   144→        Raises:
   145→            ValueError: If hybrid_norm or input_embedding_norm are enabled, as these features
   146→                are not supported in the HuggingFace config format.
   147→        """
   148→        if self.hybrid_norm or self.input_embedding_norm or self.use_qk_norm:
   149→            raise ValueError(
   150→                "Cannot export to HuggingFace format with hybrid_norm, input_embedding_norm, or use_qk_norm enabled. "

    37→class QwenConfig(LlamaConfig):
    38→    """Extends LlamaConfig with Qwen specific features"""
    39→
    40→    use_sliding_window: bool = False
    41→    sliding_window: Optional[int] = None

    44→    def __post_init__(self):

    47→        ), f"num_heads={self.num_heads} not divisible by num_kv_heads={self.num_kv_heads}."
    48→
    49→    def hf_checkpoint_converter(self, ref_checkpoint: Optional[str] = None) -> HFCheckpointConverter["QwenConfig"]:  # type: ignore
    50→        return HFCheckpointConverter(
    51→            self.__class__,
    52→            reference_checkpoint=self.reference_checkpoint if ref_checkpoint is None else ref_checkpoint,
    53→            trust_remote_code=True,
    54→            tokenizer=ref_checkpoint if self.tokenizer is None else self.tokenizer,
    55→            HfConfigClass=HfQwenConfig,
    56→        )
    57→
    58→    @classmethod

    60→        rope_theta = hf_config.rope_theta
    61→        rope_config = RotaryEmbeddingsConfig.from_hf_config(rope_theta, hf_config.rope_scaling)
    62→        return QwenConfig(
    63→            max_seq_len=hf_config.max_position_embeddings,
    64→            hidden_dim=hf_config.hidden_size,
    65→            intermediate_dim=hf_config.intermediate_size,
    66→            num_layers=hf_config.num_hidden_layers,
    67→            num_heads=hf_config.num_attention_heads,
    68→            num_kv_heads=hf_config.num_key_value_heads,
    69→            use_sliding_window=getattr(hf_config, "use_sliding_window", False),
    70→            sliding_window=getattr(hf_config, "sliding_window", None),
    71→            max_window_layers=getattr(hf_config, "max_window_layers", 0),
    72→            activation_function=ActivationFunctionEnum(hf_config.hidden_act),
    73→            initializer_range=hf_config.initializer_range,

    19→from fray.cluster import ResourceConfig

    21→
    22→def compute_per_device_parallelism(
    23→    global_batch_size: int,
    24→    microbatch_size: int,
    25→    resources: ResourceConfig,
    26→) -> int:
    27→    """Compute per_device_parallelism for gradient accumulation.
    28→
    29→    Args:
    30→        global_batch_size: The effective batch size after gradient accumulation.
    31→        microbatch_size: The batch size that fits in memory (local batch size).
    32→        resources: The ResourceConfig specifying TPU/GPU resources.
    33→
    34→    Returns:
    35→        per_device_parallelism: Number of examples each device processes per forward/backward pass.
    36→
    37→    Example:
    38→        For v5p-8 (4 chips), global_batch_size=128, microbatch_size=8:
    39→        - per_device_parallelism = 8 / 4 = 2
    40→        - gradient_accumulation = 128 / 8 = 16 steps
    41→    """
    42→    num_devices = resources.chip_count()
    43→
    44→    if microbatch_size % num_devices != 0:
    45→        raise ValueError(f"microbatch_size ({microbatch_size}) must be divisible by " f"num_devices ({num_devices})")
    46→
    47→    if global_batch_size % microbatch_size != 0:
    48→        raise ValueError(
    49→            f"global_batch_size ({global_batch_size}) must be divisible by " f"microbatch_size ({microbatch_size})"
    50→        )
    51→
    52→    per_device_parallelism = microbatch_size // num_devices
    53→    grad_accum_steps = global_batch_size // microbatch_size
    54→
    55→    print(
    56→        f"Gradient accumulation config: "
    57→        f"global_batch={global_batch_size}, microbatch={microbatch_size}, "
    58→        f"num_devices={num_devices}, per_device_parallelism={per_device_parallelism}, "
    59→        f"grad_accum_steps={grad_accum_steps}"
    60→    )
    61→
    62→    return per_device_parallelism
    63→

Perfect! Now I have all the information I need. Let me compile a comprehensive report.

## Summary: Gradient Checkpointing and Memory-Saving Options in Levanter/Marin

Based on my thorough search of the codebase, here's a comprehensive overview of how gradient checkpointing (activation checkpointing/remat) and memory-saving options are configured:

### 1. **Gradient Checkpointing Configuration**

#### Location: Model Config Level
File: `/home/kevin/marin-agentic-sft/lib/levanter/src/levanter/models/llama.py` (lines 80-81)

**In LlamaConfig:**
```python
gradient_checkpointing: bool | ScanCheckpointPolicy | str = True
scan_layers: bool = True
```

This is inherited by QwenConfig and other model configs. The field accepts three types:
- **Boolean**: Simple enable/disable
- **String**: Named policy options
- **ScanCheckpointPolicy object**: Detailed configuration

#### Policy Options (from `/home/kevin/marin-agentic-sft/lib/haliax/src/haliax/_src/scan.py`, lines 145-171)

The following string policies are supported:

| Policy | Behavior |
|--------|----------|
| `True` | **Simple checkpointing** - save outputs, don't save block internals (classic Haliax behavior) |
| `False` | **No checkpointing** - save everything, disable gradient checkpointing |
| `"offload"` | **Offload to host** - offload outputs and inputs to pinned host memory, don't save block internals |
| `"recompute"` or `"full"` | **Full remat** - don't save outputs or block internals, recompute all in backward pass |
| `"save_all"` | **Save all** - save outputs and block internals (equivalent to False) |
| `"nested"` | **Nested remat** - use nested remat with double scan; finds sqrt(num_layers) for optimal memory-compute tradeoff |

#### Real-World Usage Examples

**exp1295 (Llama-32B):**
```python
# /home/kevin/marin-agentic-sft/experiments/tootsie/exp1295_32b.py (lines 39-41)
llama_32b_remat = dataclasses.replace(
    llama_32b, gradient_checkpointing=haliax.ScanCheckpointPolicy(save_carries="offload")
)
```

**exp1395 (Qwen3-32B):**
```python
# /home/kevin/marin-agentic-sft/experiments/tootsie/exp1395_qwen3_32b.py (lines 35-37)
qwen3_32b_remat = dataclasses.replace(
    qwen3_32b, gradient_checkpointing=haliax.ScanCheckpointPolicy(save_carries="offload")
)
```

**Nested Remat (config example):**
```yaml
# /home/kevin/marin-agentic-sft/lib/levanter/config/llama_small_fast_remat.yaml (line 16)
model:
  gradient_checkpointing: "nested"
```

### 2. **ScanCheckpointPolicy Detailed Options**

File: `/home/kevin/marin-agentic-sft/lib/haliax/src/haliax/_src/scan.py` (lines 42-142)

The `ScanCheckpointPolicy` dataclass provides fine-grained control:

```python
@dataclasses.dataclass(frozen=True)
class ScanCheckpointPolicy:
    save_carries: bool | Literal["offload"] = True
    """Save carries (hidden states passed between layers). If "offload", send to pinned host memory."""
    
    save_inputs: bool | Literal["offload"] = False
    """Save non-carry inputs. If "offload", send to pinned host memory."""
    
    save_block_internals: bool | list[str] = False
    """Save internal activations of blocks. Can be a list of specific names to save."""
    
    offload_block_internals: list[str] = []
    """List of block internals to offload to host."""
    
    prevent_cse: bool = False
    """Prevent common subexpression elimination in the checkpointed function."""
    
    disable: bool = False
    """Disable gradient checkpointing entirely (for debugging)."""
    
    simple: bool = False
    """Use simple gradient checkpointing policy (no custom policy, just remat)."""
    
    nested: bool | int = False
```

## Summary: Gradient Checkpointing and Memory-Saving Options in Levanter/Marin

Based on my thorough search of the codebase, here's a comprehensive overview of how gradient checkpointing (activation checkpointing/remat) and memory-saving options are configured:

### 1. **Gradient Checkpointing Configuration**

#### Location: Model Config Level
File: `/home/kevin/marin-agentic-sft/lib/levanter/src/levanter/models/llama.py` (lines 80-81)

**In LlamaConfig:**
```python
gradient_checkpointing: bool | ScanCheckpointPolicy | str = True
scan_layers: bool = True
```

This is inherited by QwenConfig and other model configs. The field accepts three types:
- **Boolean**: Simple enable/disable
- **String**: Named policy options
- **ScanCheckpointPolicy object**: Detailed configuration

#### Policy Options (from `/home/kevin/marin-agentic-sft/lib/haliax/src/haliax/_src/scan.py`, lines 145-171)

The following string policies are supported:

| Policy | Behavior |
|--------|----------|
| `True` | **Simple checkpointing** - save outputs, don't save block internals (classic Haliax behavior) |
| `False` | **No checkpointing** - save everything, disable gradient checkpointing |
| `"offload"` | **Offload to host** - offload outputs and inputs to pinned host memory, don't save block internals |
| `"recompute"` or `"full"` | **Full remat** - don't save outputs or block internals, recompute all in backward pass |
| `"save_all"` | **Save all** - save outputs and block internals (equivalent to False) |
| `"nested"` | **Nested remat** - use nested remat with double scan; finds sqrt(num_layers) for optimal memory-compute tradeoff |

#### Real-World Usage Examples

**exp1295 (Llama-32B):**
```python
# /home/kevin/marin-agentic-sft/experiments/tootsie/exp1295_32b.py (lines 39-41)
llama_32b_remat = dataclasses.replace(
    llama_32b, gradient_checkpointing=haliax.ScanCheckpointPolicy(save_carries="offload")
)
```

**exp1395 (Qwen3-32B):**
```python
# /home/kevin/marin-agentic-sft/experiments/tootsie/exp1395_qwen3_32b.py (lines 35-37)
qwen3_32b_remat = dataclasses.replace(
    qwen3_32b, gradient_checkpointing=haliax.ScanCheckpointPolicy(save_carries="offload")
)
```

**Nested Remat (config example):**
```yaml
# /home/kevin/marin-agentic-sft/lib/levanter/config/llama_small_fast_remat.yaml (line 16)
model:
  gradient_checkpointing: "nested"
```

### 2. **ScanCheckpointPolicy Detailed Options**

File: `/home/kevin/marin-agentic-sft/lib/haliax/src/haliax/_src/scan.py` (lines 42-142)

The `ScanCheckpointPolicy` dataclass provides fine-grained control:

```python
@dataclasses.dataclass(frozen=True)
class ScanCheckpointPolicy:
    save_carries: bool | Literal["offload"] = True
    """Save carries (hidden states passed between layers). If "offload", send to pinned host memory."""
    
    save_inputs: bool | Literal["offload"] = False
    """Save non-carry inputs. If "offload", send to pinned host memory."""
    
    save_block_internals: bool | list[str] = False
    """Save internal activations of blocks. Can be a list of specific names to save."""
    
    offload_block_internals: list[str] = []
    """List of block internals to offload to host."""
    
    prevent_cse: bool = False
    """Prevent common subexpression elimination in the checkpointed function."""
    
    disable: bool = False
    """Disable gradient checkpointing entirely (for debugging)."""
    
    simple: bool = False
    """Use simple gradient checkpointing policy (no custom policy, just remat)."""
    
    nested: bool | int = False
    """Use nested remat with double scan. If True, automatically finds sqrt(num_layers)."""
```

**Example: Offloading carries to host (used for 32B models):**
```python
ScanCheckpointPolicy(save_carries="offload")
```

This reduces device memory by storing layer hidden states in host memory instead of device VRAM, trading some latency for memory savings. This is essential for large models on v4-2048 TPUs.

### 3. **Mixed Precision Configuration**

**Examples from configs:**

```yaml
# Mixed precision with bfloat16 compute
mp: p=f32,c=bfloat16

# Used in llama_small_fast.yaml

  mp: p=f32,c=bfloat16
```

This reduces memory usage by:
- **Params (p)**: float32 - maintain precision for optimizer states
- **Compute (c)**: bfloat16 - faster, less memory during forward/backward

### 4. **Quantization Support**

### 7. **Memory Reduction Hierarchy**

From most memory-efficient to most memory-hungry:

1. **Nested remat** (`"nested"`) - O(√N) memory, O(N) compute (20% slower, best for extreme memory pressure)
2. **Offload + remat** (`save_carries="offload"`) - Offload to pinned host memory, recompute in backward
3. **Simple remat** (`True`) - Save carries, recompute others (default)
4. **Full save** (`False`) - Save everything (high memory, maximum speed)

### 8. **Hardware-Specific Usage**

From the codebase patterns:

**v4-2048 TPUs (32B models):**
- Use `save_carries="offload"` for 32B models (memory-constrained)
- Mixed precision `p=f32,c=bfloat16` not always needed

**v5p TPUs (newer generation):**
- Can skip remat entirely (more HBM available)
- Example: `exp1395` runs without remat on v5p-2048:
```python
marin_32b_qwen_v5p = default_train(
    model_config=qwen3_32b,  # no remat. tons of hbm
    resources=ResourceConfig.with_tpu("v5p-2048", 1),
)
```

This comprehensive configuration system allows training large models (32B+) by trading off computation for memory in a controlled, configurable way.

Here are the options to fit 32B on v5p-32, ordered by impact:

### 1. Gradient checkpointing with offload (most likely to work)
The 32B pretraining experiments use `ScanCheckpointPolicy(save_carries="offload")` — this offloads hidden states to host memory between layers. Apply it to the model config:

```python
import haliax

model_config = dataclasses.replace(
    student_config.model_config,
    max_seq_len=32768,
    rope=DefaultRotaryEmbeddingsConfig(theta=1_000_000.0),
    cross_entropy_block_size=32000,
    gradient_checkpointing=haliax.ScanCheckpointPolicy(save_carries="offload"),
)
```

### 2. Reduce sequence length
Drop from 32768 → 16384 (what all other 32B SFT experiments use). Halves activation memory.

### 3. Nested remat
`gradient_checkpointing="nested"` — O(√N) memory at ~20% speed cost. Most aggressive option.

### 4. int8 quantized training
`SimpleSFTConfig(int8=True)` — halves parameter memory.

The **offload** option is the standard approach for 32B models in this codebase and matches the torchtune config's `enable_activation_checkpointing: True`. Want me to add it and relaunch?

"""
Fine-tunes a student model on SWE-smith agentic trajectories (Rust/ripgrep tasks).

Supports multiple teacher models:
  - gpt-5-mini:          AlienKevin/SWE-smith-rs-gpt-5-mini-trajectories                (3953 samples)
  - minimax-m2.5:        AlienKevin/SWE-smith-rs-minimax-m2.5-trajectories              (5251 samples)
  - gemini-3-flash:      AlienKevin/SWE-smith-rs-gemini-3-flash-trajectories            (1449 samples)
  - glm-4.6:             AlienKevin/SWE-smith-rs-glm-4.6-trajectories                    (369 samples)
  - swebm-minimax-m2.5:  AlienKevin/SWE-bench-multilingual-minimax-m2.5-trajectories     (299 samples)

Supports multiple student models:
  - qwen3-8b:                   Qwen/Qwen3-8B
  - qwen25-coder-32b-instruct:  Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-32B-Instruct

Each dataset has a JSON-serialized "messages" column in OpenAI chat format
plus metadata like instance_id, resolved, model, traj_id, and patch.

Usage:
    python -m experiments.exp2956_sft_swe_smith --teacher gpt-5-mini
    python -m experiments.exp2956_sft_swe_smith --teacher minimax-m2.5 --student qwen25-coder-32b-instruct
    python -m experiments.exp2956_sft_swe_smith --teacher gemini-3-flash
    python -m experiments.exp2956_sft_swe_smith --teacher glm-4.6
    python -m experiments.exp2956_sft_swe_smith --teacher swebm-minimax-m2.5

Full command for minimax-m2.5:
RAY_AUTH_MODE=token uv run lib/marin/src/marin/run/ray_run.py \\
    --env_vars WANDB_API_KEY ${WANDB_API_KEY} \\
    --env_vars WANDB_ENTITY marin-community \\
    --env_vars WANDB_PROJECT marin \\
    --env_vars TPU_CI true \\
    --env_vars HF_TOKEN ${HF_TOKEN} \\
    --cluster us-east5-a \\
    -- python -m experiments.exp2956_sft_swe_smith --teacher minimax-m2.5 --force_run_failed true
"""

import argparse
import dataclasses
import math
from dataclasses import dataclass

from levanter.data.text import ChatLmDatasetFormat
from levanter.layers.rotary import DefaultRotaryEmbeddingsConfig

from experiments.defaults import default_sft, default_tokenize
from experiments.posttrain.instruction_datasets import (
    INSTRUCTION_DATASET_NAME_TO_CONFIG,
    get_instruction_dataset,
)
from experiments.qwen2pt5_instruct_chat_template import QWEN_2_5_INSTRUCT_CHAT_TEMPLATE
from experiments.qwen3 import (
    qwen2_5_coder_32b_instruct,
    qwen2_5_coder_32b_instruct_tokenizer,
    qwen3_8b,
    qwen3_8b_tokenizer,
)
from experiments.qwen3_chat_template import QWEN_3_CHAT_TEMPLATE
from experiments.simple_sft_config import SimpleSFTConfig, compute_per_device_parallelism
from fray.cluster import ResourceConfig
from marin.execution.executor import ExecutorStep, executor_main
from marin.processing.tokenize import TokenizerStep, lm_mixture_data_config


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class TeacherConfig:
    """Configuration for a teacher model's SWE-smith trajectory dataset."""

    hf_dataset_id: str
    num_samples: int


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class StudentConfig:
    """Configuration for a student model to fine-tune."""

    model_config: object
    tokenizer: str
    model_name_or_path: str
    chat_template: str


TEACHER_CONFIGS: dict[str, TeacherConfig] = {
    "gpt-5-mini": TeacherConfig(
        hf_dataset_id="AlienKevin/SWE-smith-rs-gpt-5-mini-trajectories",
        num_samples=3953,
    ),
    "minimax-m2.5": TeacherConfig(
        hf_dataset_id="AlienKevin/SWE-smith-rs-minimax-m2.5-trajectories",
        num_samples=5251,
    ),
    "gemini-3-flash": TeacherConfig(
        hf_dataset_id="AlienKevin/SWE-smith-rs-gemini-3-flash-trajectories",
        num_samples=1449,
    ),
    "glm-4.6": TeacherConfig(
        hf_dataset_id="AlienKevin/SWE-smith-rs-glm-4.6-trajectories",
        num_samples=369,
    ),
    "swebm-minimax-m2.5": TeacherConfig(
        hf_dataset_id="AlienKevin/SWE-bench-multilingual-minimax-m2.5-trajectories",
        num_samples=299,
    ),
}

STUDENT_CONFIGS: dict[str, StudentConfig] = {
    "qwen3-8b": StudentConfig(
        model_config=qwen3_8b,
        tokenizer=qwen3_8b_tokenizer,
        model_name_or_path="Qwen/Qwen3-8B",
        chat_template=QWEN_3_CHAT_TEMPLATE,
    ),
    "qwen25-coder-32b-instruct": StudentConfig(
        model_config=qwen2_5_coder_32b_instruct,
        tokenizer=qwen2_5_coder_32b_instruct_tokenizer,
        model_name_or_path="Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-32B-Instruct",
        chat_template=QWEN_2_5_INSTRUCT_CHAT_TEMPLATE,
    ),
}

TARGET_EPOCHS = 3
TRAIN_BATCH_SIZE = 16
MICROBATCH_SIZE = 16
RESOURCES = ResourceConfig.with_tpu("v5p-32")
RESOURCE_SUFFIX = RESOURCES.device.variant.replace("-", "") if RESOURCES.device.kind == "tpu" else "gpu"


def _create_tokenization_step(
    dataset_identifier: str, short_name: str, tokenizer: str, chat_template: str
) -> TokenizerStep:
    dataset_config = INSTRUCTION_DATASET_NAME_TO_CONFIG[dataset_identifier]
    dataset = get_instruction_dataset(dataset_identifier, splits=dataset_config.splits)
    return default_tokenize(
        name=f"{short_name.split('/')[-1]}_qwen3_8b_tokenizer",
        dataset=dataset / "**/*.jsonl.gz",
        tokenizer=tokenizer,
        format=ChatLmDatasetFormat(chat_template=chat_template),
    )


def build_swe_smith_sft(teacher: str, student: str = "qwen3-8b") -> tuple[ExecutorStep, ExecutorStep]:
    """Build SFT experiment and checkpoint steps for a given teacher and student model.

    Args:
        teacher: One of the keys in TEACHER_CONFIGS.
        student: One of the keys in STUDENT_CONFIGS (default: "qwen3-8b").

    Returns:
        (sft_step, checkpoint_step) tuple.
    """
    teacher_config = TEACHER_CONFIGS[teacher]
    student_config = STUDENT_CONFIGS[student]

    model_config = dataclasses.replace(
        student_config.model_config,
        max_seq_len=32768,
        rope=DefaultRotaryEmbeddingsConfig(theta=1_000_000.0),
        cross_entropy_block_size=32000,  # Process vocab in chunks to reduce memory during loss computation
    )

    datasets = {teacher_config.hf_dataset_id: teacher_config.hf_dataset_id}
    weights = {teacher_config.hf_dataset_id: float(teacher_config.num_samples)}

    tokenized_datasets = {
        name: _create_tokenization_step(dataset_id, name, student_config.tokenizer, student_config.chat_template)
        for name, dataset_id in datasets.items()
    }

    total_examples = teacher_config.num_samples
    num_train_steps = math.ceil(TARGET_EPOCHS * total_examples / TRAIN_BATCH_SIZE)

    warmup_steps = 5
    warmup_fraction = warmup_steps / num_train_steps if num_train_steps > 0 else 0.0
    decay_fraction = 1.0 - warmup_fraction  # All post-warmup steps are cosine decay (no stable phase)

    sft_config = SimpleSFTConfig(
        resources=RESOURCES,
        tokenizer=student_config.tokenizer,
        model_name_or_path=student_config.model_name_or_path,
        train_batch_size=TRAIN_BATCH_SIZE,
        per_device_parallelism=compute_per_device_parallelism(TRAIN_BATCH_SIZE, MICROBATCH_SIZE, RESOURCES),
        per_device_eval_parallelism=8,
        num_train_steps=num_train_steps,
        learning_rate=1e-4,
        max_seq_len=32768,
        seed=42,
        steps_per_checkpoint=(total_examples // TRAIN_BATCH_SIZE) // 4,  # Every quarter epoch
        lr_schedule="cosine",
        warmup=warmup_fraction,
        decay=decay_fraction,
        weight_decay=0.01,
        beta1=0.9,
        beta2=0.999,
        epsilon=1e-8,
        pad_tokenizer_to_match_model=True,  # Model and tokenizer vocab sizes differ
    )

    mixture_config = lm_mixture_data_config(
        tokenized_datasets,
        weights,
        permutation_type="feistel",
        # IMPORTANT: Era shuffling (shuffle after every epoch). `shuffle=True` repeats the same shuffle each epoch.
        shuffle=total_examples,
        missing_weights_are_validation=True,
        mixture_block_size=12288,  # Doesn't matter for mixtures with 1 dataset
    )

    # Sanitize names for use in experiment name (e.g., "minimax-m2.5" -> "minimax_m2_5")
    teacher_slug = teacher.replace("-", "_").replace(".", "_")
    student_slug = student.replace("-", "_").replace(".", "_")

    sft_step = default_sft(
        name=f"exp2956_sft_swe_smith_{teacher_slug}_{student_slug}_32768tokens_{RESOURCE_SUFFIX}",
        tokenized=mixture_config,
        model_config=model_config,
        sft_config=sft_config,
        tags=["qwen", "swe-smith", "sft", teacher, student, RESOURCE_SUFFIX],
    )

    checkpoint = sft_step.cd(f"hf/step-{num_train_steps - 1}").nonblocking()

    return sft_step, checkpoint


# Build all teacher experiments at module level for importability (default student: qwen3-8b)
exp2956_sft_swe_smith_qwen3_8b, exp2956_checkpoint = build_swe_smith_sft("gpt-5-mini")
exp2956_sft_swe_smith_minimax_qwen3_8b, exp2956_minimax_checkpoint = build_swe_smith_sft("minimax-m2.5")
exp2956_sft_swe_smith_gemini_qwen3_8b, exp2956_gemini_checkpoint = build_swe_smith_sft("gemini-3-flash")
exp2956_sft_swe_smith_glm_qwen3_8b, exp2956_glm_checkpoint = build_swe_smith_sft("glm-4.6")
exp2956_sft_swe_smith_swebm_minimax_qwen3_8b, exp2956_swebm_minimax_checkpoint = build_swe_smith_sft(
    "swebm-minimax-m2.5"
)


if __name__ == "__main__":
    import sys

    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(description="SFT on SWE-smith trajectories")
    parser.add_argument(
        "--teacher",
        choices=list(TEACHER_CONFIGS.keys()),
        default="gpt-5-mini",
        help="Teacher model whose trajectories to train on (default: gpt-5-mini)",
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "--student",
        choices=list(STUDENT_CONFIGS.keys()),
        default="qwen3-8b",
        help="Student model to fine-tune (default: qwen3-8b)",
    )
    args, remaining = parser.parse_known_args()
    # Strip --teacher and --student from sys.argv so draccus in executor_main only sees executor args
    sys.argv = [sys.argv[0], *remaining]
    sft_step, _ = build_swe_smith_sft(args.teacher, args.student)
    executor_main(steps=[sft_step])

"""
Fine-tunes a student model on SWE-smith agentic trajectories (Rust/ripgrep tasks).

Supports multiple teacher models:
  - gpt-5-mini:          AlienKevin/SWE-smith-rs-gpt-5-mini-trajectories                (3953 samples)
  - minimax-m2.5:        AlienKevin/SWE-smith-rs-minimax-m2.5-trajectories              (5251 samples)
  - gemini-3-flash:      AlienKevin/SWE-smith-rs-gemini-3-flash-trajectories            (1449 samples)
  - glm-4.6:             AlienKevin/SWE-smith-rs-glm-4.6-trajectories                    (369 samples)
  - swebm-minimax-m2.5:  AlienKevin/SWE-bench-multilingual-minimax-m2.5-trajectories     (299 samples)

Supports multiple student models:
  - qwen3-8b:                   Qwen/Qwen3-8B
  - qwen25-coder-32b-instruct:  Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-32B-Instruct

Each dataset has a JSON-serialized "messages" column in OpenAI chat format
plus metadata like instance_id, resolved, model, traj_id, and patch.

Usage:
    python -m experiments.exp2956_sft_swe_smith --teacher gpt-5-mini
    python -m experiments.exp2956_sft_swe_smith --teacher minimax-m2.5 --student qwen25-coder-32b-instruct
    python -m experiments.exp2956_sft_swe_smith --teacher gemini-3-flash
    python -m experiments.exp2956_sft_swe_smith --teacher glm-4.6
    python -m experiments.exp2956_sft_swe_smith --teacher swebm-minimax-m2.5

Full command for minimax-m2.5:
RAY_AUTH_MODE=token uv run lib/marin/src/marin/run/ray_run.py \\
    --env_vars WANDB_API_KEY ${WANDB_API_KEY} \\
    --env_vars WANDB_ENTITY marin-community \\
    --env_vars WANDB_PROJECT marin \\
    --env_vars TPU_CI true \\
    --env_vars HF_TOKEN ${HF_TOKEN} \\
    --cluster us-east5-a \\
    -- python -m experiments.exp2956_sft_swe_smith --teacher minimax-m2.5 --force_run_failed true
"""

import argparse
import dataclasses
import math
from dataclasses import dataclass

import haliax
from levanter.data.text import ChatLmDatasetFormat
from levanter.layers.rotary import DefaultRotaryEmbeddingsConfig

from experiments.defaults import default_sft, default_tokenize
from experiments.posttrain.instruction_datasets import (
    INSTRUCTION_DATASET_NAME_TO_CONFIG,
    get_instruction_dataset,
)
from experiments.qwen2pt5_instruct_chat_template import QWEN_2_5_INSTRUCT_CHAT_TEMPLATE
from experiments.qwen3 import (
    qwen2_5_coder_32b_instruct,
    qwen2_5_coder_32b_instruct_tokenizer,
    qwen3_8b,
    qwen3_8b_tokenizer,
)
from experiments.qwen3_chat_template import QWEN_3_CHAT_TEMPLATE
from experiments.simple_sft_config import SimpleSFTConfig, compute_per_device_parallelism
from fray.cluster import ResourceConfig
from marin.execution.executor import ExecutorStep, executor_main
from marin.processing.tokenize import TokenizerStep, lm_mixture_data_config


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class TeacherConfig:
    """Configuration for a teacher model's SWE-smith trajectory dataset."""

    hf_dataset_id: str
    num_samples: int


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class StudentConfig:
    """Configuration for a student model to fine-tune."""

    model_config: object
    tokenizer: str
    model_name_or_path: str
    chat_template: str


TEACHER_CONFIGS: dict[str, TeacherConfig] = {
    "gpt-5-mini": TeacherConfig(
        hf_dataset_id="AlienKevin/SWE-smith-rs-gpt-5-mini-trajectories",
        num_samples=3953,
    ),
    "minimax-m2.5": TeacherConfig(
        hf_dataset_id="AlienKevin/SWE-smith-rs-minimax-m2.5-trajectories",
        num_samples=5251,
    ),
    "gemini-3-flash": TeacherConfig(
        hf_dataset_id="AlienKevin/SWE-smith-rs-gemini-3-flash-trajectories",
        num_samples=1449,
    ),
    "glm-4.6": TeacherConfig(
        hf_dataset_id="AlienKevin/SWE-smith-rs-glm-4.6-trajectories",
        num_samples=369,
    ),
    "swebm-minimax-m2.5": TeacherConfig(
        hf_dataset_id="AlienKevin/SWE-bench-multilingual-minimax-m2.5-trajectories",
        num_samples=299,
    ),
}

STUDENT_CONFIGS: dict[str, StudentConfig] = {
    "qwen3-8b": StudentConfig(
        model_config=qwen3_8b,
        tokenizer=qwen3_8b_tokenizer,
        model_name_or_path="Qwen/Qwen3-8B",
        chat_template=QWEN_3_CHAT_TEMPLATE,
    ),
    "qwen25-coder-32b-instruct": StudentConfig(
        model_config=qwen2_5_coder_32b_instruct,
        tokenizer=qwen2_5_coder_32b_instruct_tokenizer,
        model_name_or_path="Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-32B-Instruct",
        chat_template=QWEN_2_5_INSTRUCT_CHAT_TEMPLATE,
    ),
}

TARGET_EPOCHS = 3
TRAIN_BATCH_SIZE = 16
MICROBATCH_SIZE = 16
RESOURCES = ResourceConfig.with_tpu("v5p-32")
RESOURCE_SUFFIX = RESOURCES.device.variant.replace("-", "") if RESOURCES.device.kind == "tpu" else "gpu"


def _create_tokenization_step(
    dataset_identifier: str, short_name: str, tokenizer: str, chat_template: str
) -> TokenizerStep:
    dataset_config = INSTRUCTION_DATASET_NAME_TO_CONFIG[dataset_identifier]
    dataset = get_instruction_dataset(dataset_identifier, splits=dataset_config.splits)
    return default_tokenize(
        name=f"{short_name.split('/')[-1]}_qwen3_8b_tokenizer",
        dataset=dataset / "**/*.jsonl.gz",
        tokenizer=tokenizer,
        format=ChatLmDatasetFormat(chat_template=chat_template),
    )


def build_swe_smith_sft(teacher: str, student: str = "qwen3-8b") -> tuple[ExecutorStep, ExecutorStep]:
    """Build SFT experiment and checkpoint steps for a given teacher and student model.

    Args:
        teacher: One of the keys in TEACHER_CONFIGS.
        student: One of the keys in STUDENT_CONFIGS (default: "qwen3-8b").

    Returns:
        (sft_step, checkpoint_step) tuple.
    """
    teacher_config = TEACHER_CONFIGS[teacher]
    student_config = STUDENT_CONFIGS[student]

    model_config = dataclasses.replace(
        student_config.model_config,
        max_seq_len=32768,
        rope=DefaultRotaryEmbeddingsConfig(theta=1_000_000.0),
        cross_entropy_block_size=32000,  # Process vocab in chunks to reduce memory during loss computation
    )

    datasets = {teacher_config.hf_dataset_id: teacher_config.hf_dataset_id}
    weights = {teacher_config.hf_dataset_id: float(teacher_config.num_samples)}

    tokenized_datasets = {
        name: _create_tokenization_step(dataset_id, name, student_config.tokenizer, student_config.chat_template)
        for name, dataset_id in datasets.items()
    }

    total_examples = teacher_config.num_samples
    num_train_steps = math.ceil(TARGET_EPOCHS * total_examples / TRAIN_BATCH_SIZE)

    warmup_steps = 5
    warmup_fraction = warmup_steps / num_train_steps if num_train_steps > 0 else 0.0
    decay_fraction = 1.0 - warmup_fraction  # All post-warmup steps are cosine decay (no stable phase)

    sft_config = SimpleSFTConfig(
        resources=RESOURCES,
        tokenizer=student_config.tokenizer,
        model_name_or_path=student_config.model_name_or_path,
        train_batch_size=TRAIN_BATCH_SIZE,
        per_device_parallelism=compute_per_device_parallelism(TRAIN_BATCH_SIZE, MICROBATCH_SIZE, RESOURCES),
        per_device_eval_parallelism=8,
        num_train_steps=num_train_steps,
        learning_rate=1e-4,
        max_seq_len=32768,
        seed=42,
        steps_per_checkpoint=(total_examples // TRAIN_BATCH_SIZE) // 4,  # Every quarter epoch
        lr_schedule="cosine",
        warmup=warmup_fraction,
        decay=decay_fraction,
        weight_decay=0.01,
        beta1=0.9,
        beta2=0.999,
        epsilon=1e-8,
        pad_tokenizer_to_match_model=True,  # Model and tokenizer vocab sizes differ
    )

    mixture_config = lm_mixture_data_config(
        tokenized_datasets,
        weights,
        permutation_type="feistel",
        # IMPORTANT: Era shuffling (shuffle after every epoch). `shuffle=True` repeats the same shuffle each epoch.
        shuffle=total_examples,
        missing_weights_are_validation=True,
        mixture_block_size=12288,  # Doesn't matter for mixtures with 1 dataset
    )

    # Sanitize names for use in experiment name (e.g., "minimax-m2.5" -> "minimax_m2_5")
    teacher_slug = teacher.replace("-", "_").replace(".", "_")
    student_slug = student.replace("-", "_").replace(".", "_")

    sft_step = default_sft(
        name=f"exp2956_sft_swe_smith_{teacher_slug}_{student_slug}_32768tokens_{RESOURCE_SUFFIX}",
        tokenized=mixture_config,
        model_config=model_config,
        sft_config=sft_config,
        tags=["qwen", "swe-smith", "sft", teacher, student, RESOURCE_SUFFIX],
    )

    checkpoint = sft_step.cd(f"hf/step-{num_train_steps - 1}").nonblocking()

    return sft_step, checkpoint


# Build all teacher experiments at module level for importability (default student: qwen3-8b)
exp2956_sft_swe_smith_qwen3_8b, exp2956_checkpoint = build_swe_smith_sft("gpt-5-mini")
exp2956_sft_swe_smith_minimax_qwen3_8b, exp2956_minimax_checkpoint = build_swe_smith_sft("minimax-m2.5")
exp2956_sft_swe_smith_gemini_qwen3_8b, exp2956_gemini_checkpoint = build_swe_smith_sft("gemini-3-flash")
exp2956_sft_swe_smith_glm_qwen3_8b, exp2956_glm_checkpoint = build_swe_smith_sft("glm-4.6")
exp2956_sft_swe_smith_swebm_minimax_qwen3_8b, exp2956_swebm_minimax_checkpoint = build_swe_smith_sft(
    "swebm-minimax-m2.5"
)


if __name__ == "__main__":
    import sys

    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(description="SFT on SWE-smith trajectories")
    parser.add_argument(
        "--teacher",
        choices=list(TEACHER_CONFIGS.keys()),
        default="gpt-5-mini",
        help="Teacher model whose trajectories to train on (default: gpt-5-mini)",
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "--student",
        choices=list(STUDENT_CONFIGS.keys()),
        default="qwen3-8b",
        help="Student model to fine-tune (default: qwen3-8b)",
    )
    args, remaining = parser.parse_known_args()
    # Strip --teacher and --student from sys.argv so draccus in executor_main only sees executor args
    sys.argv = [sys.argv[0], *remaining]
    sft_step, _ = build_swe_smith_sft(args.teacher, args.student)
    executor_main(steps=[sft_step])

content.text:
Training completed successfully! Here's the summary:

- **Resumed from step 66** (checkpoint step-65 from the previous OOM run that got partway through)
- **Training finished at step 69/70** with **loss = 0.0459**
- **~4.6s per step** initially, ~6.8s/step later
- **No OOM** — gradient checkpointing with offload worked
- Now **saving the final HF checkpoint** (29 shards, ~131GB) to `gs:REDACTED`
- Currently at shard 5/29, uploading at ~80-150 MB/s (~20 min remaining for checkpoint save)

The 32B model fits on v5p-32 with `save_carries="offload"`. Training itself was fast (~7 min for the remaining steps). The checkpoint upload will take a bit longer due to the 131GB model size.

With 369 samples (glm-4.6 dataset), 3 epochs, and batch size 16:

```
steps = ceil(3 × 369 / 16) = ceil(69.19) = 70
```

| swebm-minimax-m2.5 | 299 | 57 |
| gemini-3-flash | 1,449 | 272 |
| gpt-5-mini | 3,953 | 742 |
| minimax-m2.5 | 5,251 | 985 |

70 steps is expected for this teacher/config combination. It's a small dataset — only 369 trajectories. Want to run with a larger teacher dataset like minimax-m2.5?

@@ -52,6 +52,7 @@ import dataclasses
 import math
 from dataclasses import dataclass
 
+import haliax
 from levanter.data.text import ChatLmDatasetFormat
 from levanter.layers.rotary import DefaultRotaryEmbeddingsConfig
 
@@ -168,6 +169,7 @@ def build_swe_smith_sft(teacher: str, student: str = "qwen3-8b") -> tuple[Execut
         max_seq_len=32768,
         rope=DefaultRotaryEmbeddingsConfig(theta=1_000_000.0),
         cross_entropy_block_size=32000,  # Process vocab in chunks to reduce memory during loss computation
+        gradient_checkpointing=haliax.ScanCheckpointPolicy(save_carries="offload"),
     )
 
     datasets = {teacher_config.hf_dataset_id: teacher_config.hf_dataset_id}
@@ -183,6 +185,7 @@ def build_swe_smith_sft(teacher: str, student: str = "qwen3-8b") -> tuple[Execut
 
     warmup_steps = 5
     warmup_fraction = warmup_steps / num_train_steps if num_train_steps > 0 else 0.0
+    decay_fraction = 1.0 - warmup_fraction  # All post-warmup steps are cosine decay (no stable phase)
 
     sft_config = SimpleSFTConfig(
         resources=RESOURCES,
@@ -198,6 +201,7 @@ def build_swe_smith_sft(teacher: str, student: str = "qwen3-8b") -> tuple[Execut
         steps_per_checkpoint=(total_examples // TRAIN_BATCH_SIZE) // 4,  # Every quarter epoch
         lr_schedule="cosine",
         warmup=warmup_fraction,
+        decay=decay_fraction,
         weight_decay=0.01,
         beta1=0.9,
         beta2=0.999,

6559ac7de Add swebm-minimax-m2.5 teacher for SWE-bench-multilingual SFT
a91e0ef85 Add GLM-4.6 teacher support for exp2956 SFT
41e609e8a Generalize exp2956 SFT to support multiple teacher models

input.command:
git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
Generalize exp2956 SFT to support multiple student models

- Rename exp2956_sft_swe_smith_qwen3_8b.py to exp2956_sft_swe_smith.py
- Add StudentConfig dataclass and STUDENT_CONFIGS dict with qwen3-8b
  and qwen25-coder-32b-instruct entries
- Add --student CLI argument (default: qwen3-8b)
- Add qwen2_5_coder_32b_instruct config to qwen3.py
- Align training hyperparameters with torchtune reference config:
  lr=1e-4, weight_decay=0.01, epochs=3, warmup=5 steps, beta2=0.999
- Enable gradient checkpointing with carry offload for 32B models

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"

git add experiments/qwen3.py experiments/exp2956_sft_swe_smith.py && git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
Generalize exp2956 SFT to support multiple student models

- Rename exp2956_sft_swe_smith_qwen3_8b.py to exp2956_sft_swe_smith.py
- Add StudentConfig dataclass and STUDENT_CONFIGS dict with qwen3-8b
  and qwen25-coder-32b-instruct entries
- Add --student CLI argument (default: qwen3-8b)
- Add qwen2_5_coder_32b_instruct config to qwen3.py
- Align training hyperparameters with torchtune reference config:
  lr=1e-4, weight_decay=0.01, epochs=3, warmup=5 steps, beta2=0.999
- Enable gradient checkpointing with carry offload for 32B models

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"
